next_wave_unlocked_when: each model has charter, seats, lifecycle, pressure profiles, and terminal outcome slots
sealed_commit_slot and reveal_slot remain reserved for Wave IX
no model claims live protocol or runtime effect
assistant seats remain service support and cannot become contestants
notes: Wave VIII gives Agon a session-shaped skeleton. It does not open the arena.
purpose: Define pre-protocol arena session shapes, charters, seats, lifecycle slots, pressure profile slots, and terminal outcome slots without creating live sessions.

lifecycle.purpose: Receives an Agon gate candidate from routing or trial pressure.
Defines scope, laws, seats, budgets, pressure profile, and terminal outcome slots.
Models contestants, witnesses, judge candidates, steward support, and assistant service seats.
Reserved for future sealed stance commitments. Wave VIII defines no packets.
Reserved for future reveal mechanics. Wave VIII defines no packets.
Models where lawful moves may later occur under charter limits.
Models where declared pressure profiles apply. No runtime pressure is applied.
Models future revise, stand_fast, concede, bifurcate, or defer_with_cost options.
Reserved for future verdict flow. Wave VIII issues no verdicts.
Reserved for future delta and scar bridge. Wave VIII writes no memory.
Reserved for future retention scheduling. Wave VIII schedules nothing.
Models lawful end of the session description. No runtime closeout occurs.

session_models.notes: Minimal duel-shaped model. It is the future kernel shape, not a runtime session.

pressure_profiles.purpose: Models timebox pressure without runtime timer.
Models required prediction before future reveal.

steward.purpose: Model budget and stop-line steward support without arena sovereignty.

witnesses.purpose: Model chronicle requirement without durable memory writes.

Future outcome slot for honest branch split.
Future outcome slot for unresolved pressure with explicit cost.

Future outcome slot for isolation pending owner review.
